title: Uniface Messages 8000-8999
text: Uniface Messages 8000-8999 Refer to the Uniface message number, message text, and an explanation of the message. The following table refers to messages with numbering between 8000 - 8999 . Search: Column Message number Message text Explanation Filter results... 115 rows found Message number Message text Explanation Filter 115 rows found Filter 115 rows found Filter 115 rows found Message number Message text Explanation 8001 [debug] not (all) field data loaded yet. This message appears while the application shell is in Debug mode. You tried to examine a field that does not contain data. This is probably because the occurrence has not yet been fetched. Execute a few more lines with step and try again. 8002 [debug] no field data available. This message appears while the application shell is in Debug mode. You tried to examine a field that does not contains data. 8003 Open error on assignment table [table name]. Uniface failed to access the specified assignment file. Check if the logical assignment file defined in the application definition is available. If not, create the table or define the correct table. 8004 Open error on keyboard translation table [table name]. Uniface failed to access the specified keyboard translation table for keyboard input. The attempt to access it may be unauthorized. Check the authorizations for your application shell. Otherwise, check if the keyboard translation table defined in the application definition is in associated library. If several projects each use a different library, the relevant libraries must all be updated when a source definition is modified. Uniface generates this error when it cannot find the runtime library. Ensure that the $UUU setting in your assignment file points to the correct location of runtime library. 8005 Open error on display device table [table name]. Uniface failed to access the specified device table for display output. This can occur if the table is not available or if it was not compiled in Uniface 9. You can: Check whether the table defined in the application definition is available. If not, create the device table or define the correct one. Compile the translation table, then try again. 8006 No text available with ID [id_string]. A text block identified by $text was referred to in a ProcScript module that could not be located in the runtime library file. Ensure that a message is present for the current variation code and the id string used in the ProcScript module. If the text block is present, check your system settings. If you think the message comes from Uniface, look up the id number in the Uniface Library. 8007 Excess characters; enter a shorter string or no string. Enter an acceptable number of characters or leave empty. 8008 Assignment statement error: %%$sub = %%$sub(1). The assignment statement contains incorrect syntax or elements that are not supported in your current Uniface installation. For example, a driver in the assignment is not included in the SEK (Software Enable Key). 8009 Assignment table error: not enough memory available for paths %%$sub. Uniface could not claim sufficient memory from the operating system. This is serious, since the assignment files are among the first to be loaded. This problem is therefore more likely to be an operating system fault. Check the operating system. 8010 Memory allocation error on display definition. Uniface could not claim sufficient memory from the operating system. Check the operating system or contact your system administrator. 8011 License not available. Uniface was not installed correctly, or the license information was deleted by mistake. Run the installation procedure again, ensuring that the correct license information is entered, or contact your Uniface representative. 8012 Multiple definitions for PATH via OPEN or /LOG request. You defined a path and then tried to use an open instruction or /log request with parameters already defined in the path. 8013 Minimum space required not available from Operating System. The minimum space required to perform the action you just initiated is not available from the operating system. Contact your system administrator. 8014 Environment directory-assignment USYS is not set. To set up the environment for Uniface, you must set the USYS environment variable. Refer to the relevant installation guide for the correct procedure. 8015 Pathname for USYS or IDE is not correct. To set up the environment for Uniface, you must set th USYS environment variable. Refer to the relevant installation guide for the correct procedure. 8016 Illegal keyboard input code found. Uniface detected an incorrect definition in your keyboard translation table. 8017 Claimed control area size %%size on table %%$entname larger than maximum of 8192 bytes. If the $cb_virt assignment is set to TRUE, the DBMS control block cannot be larger than eight bytes. 8018 Link failure seen: module % not found. While attempting to call a 3GL routine, Uniface detected a missing module that was not linked in. Check if all objects are defined in your application definition. 8019 Divide by zero. A ProcScript statement contains a computation that divides by zero. Check that the ProcScript operand is initialized or remove the zero from your calculation. 8020 Fetch error: hit not available. This is an internal error. Contact your Uniface representative. 8021 Application shell failed cannot connect to window manager %%$gui. Your application shell cannot be started because Uniface cannot connect to the specified window manager. Check your assignment file. 8022 Existing record not replaced. The Copy facility tried to insert a record, but it already exists. If the existing record or records should be replaced, click Supersede and try again. 8023 Error during WRITE (disk space error?). The copy facility detected an error while writing. This is probably due to insufficient space on disk. Check if disk space is the problem. If so, delete some files and try again. This message can also be displayed when: using the /cpy switch using the /ins switch using the import or export facilities This can be due to NULL values or duplicate keys. Check the definitions that you are attempting to convert, import, or export. 8024 Copied now….%%$sub records%%%. Displays the number of records currently copied. 8025 Copied now….%%$sub records, skipped %%$sub(1) records. The Copy facility detected an inconsistency in the input file during the copy operation. This could be due to a corrupted row or record. The number of records copied, and the number of records not copied, are shown. 8026 Input file %%$sub does not exist. The input file was not found. Check the name of the input file or table, or discover why Uniface cannot find it. Correct the error and try again. 8027 Output file %%$sub already exists. Specify another name if you do not want to overwrite the existing file. 8028 Overflow of output file %%$sub already exists. The Copy facility found that an overflow file ( O EntityName ) is already present for the file or table to be copied. Delete the file or table and try again, or specify another name. 8029 Cannot open output file %%$sub%%%. The operating system does not allow the Copy facility to open the output file or table. Check why the file or table cannot be opened, for example the directory may be full, insufficient privileges may be present, or the path name may be incorrect. Correct the error and try again. 8030 Exceptional error %%$sub while copying file %%$sub(1)%%%. The Copy facility detected an error that it could not interpret further. The error code corresponds with the Uniface I/O error codes. Correct the error and try again. Refer to the "I/O Message Levels" topic. 8031 Entities selected for copy: %%$sub, copied %%$sub(1)%%%. The ‘entities selected’ number indicates the number of entities in the library that match the select profile. The ‘copied’ number shows the number of entities with records copied into them. 8032 Entity %%$sub Total copied: %%$sub(1) records/rows. Displays the number of records copied to the specified entity. 8033 Specify model name to copy entity %%$sub%%%. The application model of the entity was not completely specified, and the Copy facility found more than one record with this entity name. Enter the application model name and try again. 8034 Input file of older version; cannot copy. TRX files created with Uniface Version 3.3 or lower cannot be copied when running Uniface Version 4.0 or higher. The TRX files must be created with the current version before they are copied. 8035 Fixed part of table exceeds DBMS connector limits. The length of the fixed part of the record is larger than the limit for your DBMS. The fixed size of the table depends on the number of fields in the entity, because Uniface reserves space for each field in the entity. It does not depend on the actual content of each field. 8040 Are you sure you want to exit? Your data is not saved if you click OK to exit the application. 8041 Assignment error: no section header for “%%$sub” = “%%$sub(1)”. Insert the appropriate section header in front of the specified assignment statement. Section headers are mandatory in assignment files. 8057 Please specify application to be started. For some operating systems, you must specify the name of the application you want to start. Enter the name. 8058 Network connection terminated. The network failed. 8059 Reference made to excluded field: %%$sub. The component references a field that is not included in the component’s field list. Add the field to the field list of the current component. 8060 Insufficient LOGON information: Uniface Server not available for path %%$sub. The Uniface Server requires logon information from the client. When the Uniface Server is running in batch mode, you can provide this information by modifying the assignment file on the client or server machine. 8061 Pipe broken detected to next server; forced exit. The connection to a client has broken and at least two PolyServers are running. The problem could occur at some location between the PolyServers, the network, or the server machine. The system aborts. 8062 [L] Request terminated (network failure (0) detected). The client logon to the server failed due to a network error. Contact your system administrator. 8064 Server handshaking error; server is incompatible with client. A compatibility error exists between the client and the Uniface Server or between the Uniface Server and the Uniface Router. Check that you are running the same versions of Uniface router/server software. 8068 Copy failed: open error on output file/table filename. Uniface could not copy to the file or table you specified. Check that you have entered the correct path, as specified in your assignment file. 8101 Please enter data in current field. You must enter data in the current field. 8102 No information available via URouter. There is no information available via URouter. 8103 User name and/or password not valid for Authorize request. The user name and/or password you entered are not valid for privileged-operation authorization. The user name and password are authenticated on the machine running the URouter, so they must represent a valid account on that machine. 8104 Disconnect failed. Self-explanatory. 8105 Unauthorize failed. Self-explanatory. 8106 Current settings will not be saved in URMON.CNF You have chosen not to save the monitor's current settings. 8107 Profile characters detected. Uniface-style profile characters may not be used. Data will be used as entered. 8108 URouter Monitor and URouter have incompatible versions. The Router Monitor and Uniface Router have incompatible versions. 8116 Unexpected network failure. Unexpected network failure. 8117 Hostname not valid. The host name you entered is not valid. 8118 Connection to URouter failed. The connection to the Uniface Router has failed. 8120 Maxclients or maxmons exceeded for URouter. The maximum number of clients or Router Monitors for the URouter has been exceeded. 8121 No permission for this action on URouter. You are not authorized to perform this action on the URouter. 8152 Protocol error (invalid call sequence). Protocol error (invalid call sequence). You attempted to connect when already connected, or called an operation when not connected. 8198 Close this list? Self-explanatory. 8199 Close URouter Monitor? Self-explanatory. 8202 Error in info field #%%$sub%%%. The value in information field #I was not recognized. You must use valid values for this field. 8203 Error in application model, entity or field name ‘%%$sub’. You used incorrect syntax to name the object. See the CASE Bridge Cookbook for name syntax rules. 8204 Syntax error: %%$sub not allowed in this position. The specified object is an illegal character. 8205 Info code #%%$sub is illegal. You entered an illegal information code. Specify the correct code to continue. 8206 Info field #%%$sub is too long. You entered a code that exceeds the maximum field length allowed. Specify the correct values. 8207 Field name list #F is illegal. A syntax error occurred in the field name list. Ensure that field names are separated by a comma ( , ). 8209 Info field #%%$sub must be numeric. You entered non-digital characters in a numeric field. 8211 Record specification %%$sub is illegal. You entered incorrect syntax for the record specification of an entity, key or relationship. Ensure that your record specification is correct, and continue. 8212 Cannot %%$$UP_MESS1 %%$$UP_MESS2. No entity specified. You entered a field or key record minus the entity name (. EntityName ). You must specify an entity in the field or key specification. 8213 Identical names for one and many entities not allowed. This message is only relevant for relationship records. Change one of the entity names. 8214 Enter a model name for automatic update. If you selected automatic update, enter the name of the target model. 8218 Close without synchronizing? To end the synchronize session without synchronizing, click OK . To continue with the synchronization procedure, click Cancel . 8219 Cannot open file ‘%%$$UP_MESS1’. This message indicates one of the following: The file is locked. The file does not exist. The file name was incorrect. A disk read error occurred. The file is write protected. 8220 Records (for insert) without information were found, include (Y/N)? The selected differences include one or more records that do not contain record information and ProcScript information. If you want Uniface to provide such records with default information before inserting them, click ‘Y’, otherwise, click ‘N’. 8223 Clean can only be used on application models and entities. You cannot use the modification code C (Clean) for key, field or relationship records. 8224 Cannot %%$$UP_MESS1, %%$$UP_MESS2 does not exist. The object you tried to load was not found. Check that you entered the name correctly. 8225 Cannot %%$$UP_MESS1, %%$$UP_MESS2 already exists. You cannot insert an existing object. Consult the CASE Bridge Cookbook for more information about the use of insertion codes. 8226 No field names specified (info field #F). You did not specify the list of field names that comprise the key in a relationship or key record. Specify the list of field names. 8227 Field and or key sequence numbers have been renumbered (entity %%$$UP_MESS1). Fields or keys with the same sequence numbers were renumbered. This occurs if numbers are missing from the sequence. You cannot influence this renumbering. If the sequence is important, try again. 8228 Relationship must contain two entities. Check the entity names specified for the relationship record. 8229 Cannot %%$$UP_MESS1 %%$$UP_MESS2. Subtype or unknown entity found. The entity specified in the F(Field) or K(Key) record was either subtype or was not found. Check the entity name and ensure that the entity is not a subtype. 8235 Proc syntax error found. A syntax error occurred in the ProcScript. Review the syntax in the documentation for the ProcScript instructions. Refer to the "Uniface Reference" topic. 8237 Cannot copy definitions from field template %%$$UP_MESS1. Specify the field template on a previous line of the CIF file. The field template must also exist in the repository before it can be used as a template. 8238 Cannot %%$$UPPMESS1 %%$$UP_MESS2. Links still exist. You cannot delete the following: A model or entity that is used in a relationship or that has entities, subentities or fields connected to it. A template (field, entity, field interface, field layout or field syntax) that is referred to in another record. To delete a model, including all entities and fields, use the R (Remove) modification code. To delete a template, remove all references to it. 8239 One or many entity does not exist. You cannot create a relationship when either the one or the many entity does not exist. Check the entities defined in the relationship. 8240 Field sequence number 0 only allowed if not in database. You cannot assign the field sequence number 0 to a database field. You can only assign 0 for non-database fields. 8245 %%$$UP_MESS1 %%$$UP_MESS2 not found. The specified template was not found. 8246 %%$$UP_MESS1 %%$$UP_MESS2 already exists as a subtype of entity %%$$UP_MESS3. The entity or subtype already exists as a subtype in this application model. The entity and subtype names must be unique within the application model. 8248 No difference found. No differences between the models were detected. 8249 Compiler found errors in %%$$UP_MESS1 procs. The compiler found errors in the listed global procs that you edited in the Global Procs form. 8250 Compiler found syntax error(s) in ProcScript. This message is self-explanatory. 8300 Please exit the debugger through the menu. Choose File > Exit to exit the debugger and application. 8302 The application is already running. You attempted to start an application that was already running. 8303 Invalid target for breakpoint. The current line in the code pane cannot contain a breakpoint. 8305 Illegal reset context. This message is self-explanatory. 8307 You must select at least one watch. Select at least one watch. 8308 A watch can be set for fields and variables only. You can set a watch only for fields, global variables, component variables, local variables, and Uniface functions ( $FunctionName ). 8309 New breakpoints have no effect until the application is halted. This message is self-explanatory. 8310 Unable to show help (%%$status). No help is available for the current topic. 8312 Code received for unexpected module. The code pane is out of sync with the selected module. This usually happens when you are scrolling quickly through the modules using the scroll bar, and the debugger cannot display the modules at the same rate. 8313 No code available for this module. The debugger cannot supply code for the current module. 8314 AddWatch invalid in browser. An internal program error has occurred, and any AddWatch commands will fail. 8315 Component cannot be browsed. The browser cannot open the component you specified. This message usually displays when you attempt to open an application in the browser. 8316 Library cannot be browsed. The browser cannot open the library that you specified. This message usually displays when you attempt to open a library that is not loaded by the application. 8317 Menu cannot be browsed. The browser cannot open the menu that you specified. 8320 Open failed: %%$status. The value of $status indicates why the open command failed. 8323 Processing component information… This message is self-explanatory. 8324 Unknown message: %%$msgId%%%. The debugger cannot find the message with the specified Id. 8326 Retrieving components, please wait… This message is self-explanatory. 8328 %%item(“DESCRIPTION”, $procErrorContext)%%% [%%item(“COMPONENTNAME”, item(“ADDITIONAL”, $procErrorContext))]. An error occurred when a debugger component was being activated. To fix this, you usually need to change your assignment settings. 8329 Application is running: cannot refresh context. During debugging, you cannot load new data for component name, component variables, entities, and available fields while the application is running. 8340 Debugger configuration file (UDBG.CNF) has wrong header. The first text line of the debugger’s preferences file UDBG.CNF (usually found in the working directory) does not read 'Configuration file UNIFACE Debugger'. This indicates that the file was modified by another application. Check to see if the file has is corrupted. If so, resave it from within the debugger. 8341 Saving configuration file to disk failed. The debugger‘s preferences file UDBG.CNF (usually found in the working directory) could not be created or updated. Possible causes: the file is locked by another application, or there is insufficient user rights to perform this action. 8500 %%$$UP_MESS1 failed for Personal Query/Personal Access. Check the Define Lists form ( Customization > System Lists ) for Personal Query/ Access. Then enter the valid spawn command for the operating system. Related Links I/O Message Levels Most of the messages that display in the message frame are determined by the I/O message level. The I/O message is a number that is determined by adding up the codes of the applicable message types. Uniface Reference The Uniface Reference collection assembles all reference documentation in one place for easy access, including ProcScript language instructions, API calls, trigger and widget descriptions, messages, settings, and so on.
